column12: The State Department for Roads suffered a Ksh 33 billion underfunding which severely crippled road maintenance and development.
111 employees earned less than a third of their basic salary in breach of the Employment Act exposing them to financial risk.
The Department holds Ksh 5.6 million in outstanding payables which could attract avoidable interest and penalties.
The Department unlawfully forfeited 1216 leave days for 79 staff members violating employment laws and court precedents.
Only 1 percent of the workforce are persons with disabilities falling far below the legal 5 percent minimum threshold.
Ksh 16.5 million belonging to Kenya Railways retirees has been held for over five years instead of being surrendered.
A Ksh 130 million Transport Data Centre remains incomplete 10 years after its deadline risking equipment obsolescence.

column12: 36 employees were allowed to earn less than a third of their basic pay in violation of employment regulations.

column12: Construction of Cluster 51 markets stalled at 15 percent completion despite 100 percent of the time elapsing wasting taxpayer money.
